logline: So, there was this guy CHARACTER_A, right? He lived in this drafty third-floor apartment that always smelled vaguely like old cabbage and wet wool. One Tuesday, he was trying to fix his toaster with a butter knife—don't ask me why. That's when CHARACTER_B knocked on the door, holding a giant, neon-green plastic flamingo. Now, CHARACTER_A didn't really know CHARACTER_B all that well, they just shared a landlord who was never around. CHARACTER_B says, "Hey, can you keep this in your tub for a couple of hours? The plumbing's acting up." CHARACTER_A was too tired to argue, so he just pointed toward the bathroom and went back to his toaster. But about ten minutes later, he hears this weird clicking noise coming from the tub. He walks in, and CHARACTER_B is sitting on the edge of the tub, feeding dried corn to the flamingo. Except it wasn't a plastic flamingo at all, it was a real, actual bird that CHARACTER_B had dyed green. I have no idea where you even buy green dye for a bird, or why you'd want to. CHARACTER_B just looked up and said, "Don't tell Mrs. Gable, she thinks it's a very large parrot." CHARACTER_A just stood there, holding his butter knife, thinking about his life choices. He decided right then and there that fixing the toaster was a lost cause anyway. So he sat down on the bathmat, and they both just watched the bird peck at the porcelain. It turned out CHARACTER_B had rescued it from some weird roadside zoo that went bankrupt. They spent the rest of the evening eating cold pizza and trying to teach it to mimic the microwave beep. By midnight, the bird actually did a pretty good impression, which drove the neighbor's dog completely nuts. CHARACTER_B ended up leaving around two in the morning, taking the green bird and the pizza box with them. And honestly, CHARACTER_A never did get that toaster working, but he still keeps a bag of corn in his cabinet.
themes: The Absurdity of Apartment Living: The strange ways neighbors interact and get dragged into each other's chaotic lives simply due to shared walls and bad plumbing.
Giving In to Chaos: The moment CHARACTER_A abandons his frustrating, mundane task (fixing the toaster) to participate in something completely bizarre and useless.
Accidental Connection: How two relative strangers can bond over something completely ridiculous like teaching a dyed flamingo to sound like a microwave.
points: The Haircut Incident: Last summer, CHARACTER_A tried to trim CHARACTER_B’s hair using kitchen shears and a metal mixing bowl as a guide. It went so horribly wrong that CHARACTER_B had to wear a beanie indoors for a month and still refuses to let CHARACTER_A hold scissors.

The Haunted Radiator: CHARACTER_B once successfully convinced CHARACTER_A that their building’s clanking radiator was haunted by the ghost of a Victorian child. CHARACTER_A actually left a small offering of shiny copper pennies on it every Tuesday for six weeks before realizing CHARACTER_B was pocketing them.

The Stairwell Sofa: The time they tried to move a heavy velvet sofa down three flights of stairs, got it completely wedged at a 45-degree angle in the narrow hallway, and ended up just leaving it there for a week with a sign that read "Communal Bench—Enjoy the View."
